text:
Admission Date:  2017-09-12              Discharge Date:   1975-04-24

Date of Birth:  1988-10-03             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Kelly
Chief Complaint:
SOB/CP

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Pericardial drain
Thoracentesis X2

History of Present Illness:
55yo F with h/o recurrent metastatic stage III endometrial CA
s/p radiation and chemo (carboplatin and taxol) with cc of SOB.
Patient was diagnosed one year ago with metastatic endometrial
CA. Underwent XRT and chemo with carboplatin and taxol. Had
finished treatment and then on recent visit to OB/GYN CA-125
level was elevated from 18->77. Patient was in the process of
being worked up for recurrence of her CA.
.
She began having SOB x 3-4 days. She presented to OSH today and
had CT scan that showed larged pericardial effusion and
bilateral pleural effustions. She was transferred to Fort Leonard Wood Army Community Hospital ED
for management of pericardial effusion.
.
In the ED at Fort Leonard Wood Army Community Hospital patient complained of 05-20 chest pain like a
heaviness in her chest. VS 99.3, 113bpm, BP 107/67, 18, 98% on
4L NC. Then patient had BP 88/77. Received 600mL NS which
improved the BP to 135/60. A TTE was done that showed large
pericardial effusion with signs of tamponade including
ventricular collapse. She was taken to the cath lab and 500mL
bloody fluid removed.
.
On presentation to the floor she said her SOB was much better.
she still c/o a CP of abt 05-10.
.
On review of systems, she denies any prior history of stroke,
TIA, deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, bleeding at the
time of surgery, myalgias, joint pains, cough, hemoptysis, black
stools or red stools. She denies recent fevers, chills or
rigors. She denies exertional buttock or calf pain. All of the
other review of systems were negative.
.
Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of paroxysmal
nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, ankle edema, palpitations, syncope
or presyncope.
.
.

Brief Hospital Course:
55yo F with h/o recurrent metastatic uterine CA admitted with
pericardial effusion and tamponade s/p pericardial drain
placement.

# Pericardial Effusion:  Most likely etiology of the patient's
effusions is related to her history of cancer.  The patient was
taken to the cath lab for a pericardial drain placement.  In the
cath lab, 500 cc of bloody fluid was removed. The patient did
not have a history of recent URI.  The pericardial drain was
able to be pulled the day after placement as residual output was
minimal. Repeat echo was negative for effusion.  The fluid was
sent for culture, cell count and cytology and were pending at
the time of discharge. The patient was given NSAIDs for pain
control.  She remained stable from this prespective during her
stay. She will need a follow up echo in 4 weeks (ordered in
OMR).

# Pleural Effusions:  Bilateral pleural effusions also thought
to be related to malignancy.  Left sided thoracentesis was done
on 2005-04-08 without complication and improvement of dyspnea(-1Lt
exudatve fluid). Right sided thoracentesis was done on 1998-09-10
without complications (-0.8Lt exudatve fluid).  Both fluid
analysis showed exudative fluid.  Cytology and culture were
pending at time of discharge and will be followed up by her
oncocologist. Subsequent CXR were possitive for reaccumulation
of fluid. Interventional pulmonology was consulted and felt the
patient may benefit from pleurodesis or another procedure to
keep her effusions from recurring. They will see her in the
office 2 days after discharge to discuss this with her.

# Tachycardia: Most likely tachycardia is related to pain,
dehydration and malignancy.  Most likely sinus tachycardia
however could be atrial tach vs aflutter. Controlled pain.
Gently IV fluids for dehydration were given.  Metoprolol was
given PRN.

#. Endometrial Cancer:  Poorly differentiated stage IIIC
endometrial cancer, now with
metastases to the lungs and liver.  Consulted Onc while the
patient was admitted. The patient has refused chemotherapy in
the past and continued to want alternative treatments instead of
more rounds of chemotherapy.

#. Hyponatremia: Remained stable during the hospitalization.

# PUMP: normal EF of 55% on echo to evaluate pericardial
effusion.

# CORONARIES: No h/o coronary disease


Medications on Admission:
MVI
Vit D

1. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed.
2. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
3. Metoprolol Tartrate 25 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID
(2 times a day).
Disp:*60 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
4. Ibuprofen 400 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q8H (every 8
hours) as needed.


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Acadia-st. Landry Hospital VNA

Discharge Diagnosis:
Primary Diagnoses:
Pericardial effusion
Pleural effusions
Endometrial Cancer


Discharge Condition:
The patient was hemodynamically stable prior to discharge.


Discharge Instructions:
You were admitted to Fort Leonard Wood Army Community Hospital for evaluation of shortness of
breath.  You were found to have fluid around your heart and
around both of your lungs.  You underwent procedures to have
these collections of fluid drained.  There were no complications
from those procedures.  If you experience pain from the
procedures, you should take ibuprofen over the counter.  You
will be seen by Interventional Pulmonary to discuss possible
more permanent treatment of the fluid around your lungs.
.
You were also found to have a fast heart rate.  You were started
on a new medication for this fast heart rate.  Please continue
to take this medication on discharge.
.
New Medications:
Metoprolol 25mg twice a day
Ibuprofen 400mg every 8 hours as needed for pain (can take over
the counter ibuprofen as well)
.
If you experience worsening shortness of breath, chest pain,
worsening cough, fever, chills or any other worrisome symptoms
please seek medical attention.

Followup Instructions:

11:00am.  Emergency Department, Inside Emergency Department Building, 01408 Compton Estates
East Judy, KY 82038 Children's Hospital Colorado At Memorial Hospital Central
116. This is to evaluate your lung fluid collections and to
discuss further treatments. The number to reschedule the
appointment is (529-522-5593.
.

.

.

scheduled here but can be done at another facility through Dr.
Simpson if you want.
